name: matrix-2x2
description: Static 2×2 quadrant grid with author-placed items per cell.
purpose: Use for categorical 2×2 reasoning when the items are fixed and you control which cell each lands in. For data-plotted scatter on continuous axes, use quadrant instead.

axes.description: Four outer list items (one per cell). Lead each with **Quadrant label.** then the items as inner bullets.

skeleton:
<!-- _class: matrix-2x2 -->

## Where each option lives.

- **High value · Low cost.**
  - First item in this quadrant
  - Second item
- **High value · High cost.**
  - First item in this quadrant
- **Low value · Low cost.**
  - First item in this quadrant
- **Low value · High cost.**
  - First item in this quadrant
sample:
<!-- _class: matrix-2x2 -->

## Where the transformation bets land on effort and impact.

- **High impact · Low effort.**
  - Weekly signal review
  - Decision-log template
- **High impact · High effort.**
  - Scoring-model rewrite
  - Org-wide rollout
- **Low impact · Low effort.**
  - Renaming the framework, again
  - A new Confluence space
- **Low impact · High effort.**
  - Dashboards nobody opens
  - The maturity-model self-assessment

whenToUse.title: Categorical 2×2 reasoning
whenToUse.body: SWOT, Eisenhower, BCG growth-share, risk × impact, build-vs-buy. The two axes are discrete labels and you place each item by judgement.

whenToUse.title: Fixed, author-placed items
whenToUse.body: You know which cell each item belongs in and the placement is the editorial argument. Data-plotted scatter on continuous axes belongs in `quadrant`.

whenToUse.title: Bottom-right accent matters
whenToUse.body: The fourth cell carries the accent ring as the conventional outcome or high-priority quadrant. Place the items you want emphasised there.

antiPatterns.title: Continuous-axis data
antiPatterns.body: If items have x/y coordinates rather than quadrant labels, use `quadrant`. matrix-2x2 is author-placed categorical, not plotted.

antiPatterns.title: Empty quadrants left blank
antiPatterns.body: An empty cell still needs a label or an explicit (none) placeholder. A missing card breaks the 2×2 symmetry.

antiPatterns.title: More than 4 items per cell
antiPatterns.body: Each quadrant holds 1–4 items. Past that the cells crowd. Promote inner items to their own slide if needed.

related.name: quadrant
related.when: items have continuous x/y coordinates rather than discrete quadrant labels

related.name: verdict-grid
related.when: options scored across more than two dimensions

related.name: obligation-matrix
related.when: many rows × many columns of state-marker cells

related.name: cards-grid
related.when: the items don't divide along two axes
